meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Master Your Codebase as a Student
content.seo_description: Overwhelmed by AI tools? Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, build better projects, and accelerate your engineering career.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting your IDE. By integrating Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for real-time CLI-based debugging, you transform AI from a chaotic distraction into a surgical precision instrument. This workflow allows you to diagnose complex stack traces, refactor technical debt, and ship production-ready features with total confidence.
content.intro:
You stare at the screen, a mountain of AI-generated snippets cluttering your workspace, your logic tangled in a web of hallucinated dependencies. The promise of 'AI-assisted coding' feels more like a frantic game of whack-a-mole, where every fix introduces two new bugs. You aren't just coding anymore; you are managing a chaotic digital ecosystem that feels miles beyond your grasp.

But the chaos is an illusion. The shift from 'AI-dependent' to 'AI-architect' happens the moment you stop treating models like search engines and start treating them like junior developers. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity’s structural analysis and Claude Code’s CLI-native execution, you reclaim your focus and turn your IDE into a high-performance laboratory.

steps.title: Contextualizing the Architecture
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, you must map the terrain. Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies and identify where your current logic deviates from best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project architecture. Identify circular dependencies and suggest a modular refactor for the core business logic.

steps.title: Surgical Error Diagnosis
steps.description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Pipe your stderr output directly into Claude Code to get a root-cause analysis based on your specific file tree.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic flow of this specific module and why it is failing to handle async state updates.

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Once the bug is identified, use Claude Code to apply the fix across multiple files while maintaining code style consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor these functions to use clean error handling patterns while keeping existing unit tests passing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/utils.ts --prompt 'Apply error handling pattern'

steps.title: Validating Against Standards
steps.description: Ensure your fixes didn't break the build. Use Antigravity to verify that your new code adheres to the project's established design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify this new implementation against the established project design patterns and check for performance bottlenecks.

steps.title: Documentation Generation
steps.description: Never leave a feature undocumented. Use the context captured during debugging to generate professional-grade README updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate technical documentation for the changes made, focusing on the resolution of the identified bug.

pro_tips: Treat the Claude Code CLI as your primary terminal window, not just a side-chat; keeping it in-context with your file system is the secret to accuracy.
Use Antigravity to generate 'architectural snapshots' before every major refactor so you can always revert to a known-stable state.
Always run your own unit tests before accepting a Claude Code suggestion; AI can be confident even when it is factually incorrect.
Keep your prompt history lean; clear your Claude session state when shifting from 'debugging' to 'feature development' to prevent cross-contamination of logic.
Use CLI aliases for your most frequent AI-debug commands to maintain flow state and minimize context switching.
content.next_steps: Now that you have optimized your debugging loop, integrate a CI/CD pipeline like GitHub Actions to automate these tests, ensuring your AI-assisted code remains production-ready 24/7.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to stop being overwhelmed and start being an architect. Don't just code—engineer your future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
